name: Bond Loans
description: A Bond Loan is an interest-free loan to cover the rental bond when moving into private rental accommodation.

name: Public and community housing
description: Public and community housing is available to assist vulnerable people who are assessed as having the greatest need.

name: RentConnect
description: Get help finding and applying for a rental (RentConnect)

name: RentConnect
description: Get help staying in your rental (RentConnect)

name: Rental Grants
description: A Rental Grant is a one-off grant of 2 weeks rent to help you move into private rental accommodation. A Rental Grant is for people in a housing crisis. This grant does not have to be repaid.

name: Queensland Housing Finance Loan
description: The Queensland Housing Finance Loan helps people who can afford to buy or build a home but cannot get private finance from a bank or building society.

name: Queensland State Housing Loan
description: The Queensland State Housing Loan allows some public housing tenants to purchase their rental property.

name: Queensland first home owner grant
description: The Queensland first home owner grant helps eligible first-time home buyers to build or buy a new home or a [substantially renovated home](https://qro.qld.gov.au/property-concessions-grants/first-home-grant/eligibility/#substantial-renovation).

name: Mortgage Relief Loan
description: The Mortgage Relief Loan helps people pay their mortgage during short term periods of illness or unemployment.

name: Queensland Community Support Scheme
description: The Queensland Community Support Scheme (QCSS) can help you to continue living safely in your home and actively participate in your community.

name: Retirement village and park information
description: The Queensland Retirement Village and Park Advice Service can provide you with free specialist advice and information if you are living in, or are considering living in, a manufactured home park or retirement village.

name: Youth accommodation support
description: Youth accommodation support provides helpful information about finding housing and accommodation, moving out of home, and support if you're at risk of homelessness.

name: Youth Housing and Reintegration Services (YHARS)
description: Youth Housing and Reintegration Services (YHARS) help young people who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless. They provide support, financial help and access to different accommodation options.

name: My Aged Care
description: My Aged Care provides information about disability, aged care and other support services for people who are 65 years or older and their carers.

name: Aged care homes
description: Aged care homes can provide quality care and services in a safe environment if you are no longer able to live in your own home.

name: Sharing your home
description: Homesharing or co-residency matches people who need practical help to live at home with people who need or can offer accommodation.

name: Using assistive technology
description: Assistive technology is equipment or devices that help you do things you can’t do because of your disability. Assistive technology may also help you do something more easily or safely.

name: National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS)
description: The NDIS funds some home and living supports for eligible people with disability.

name: Summer Foundation Housing Toolkit
description: The Summer Foundation has worked closely with people with disability and their families to understand the experiences, issues and concerns about housing and support. Throughout this consultation process, the aim was to create a toolkit that is relevant and user-friendly, to help people make their own choices. The toolkit is for both people with disability who require a high level of support and their families.

name: Please contact us
description: Thank you for using the Queensland Housing Services Finder. Given the information you provided about your residency status, we recommend you contact your nearest [Housing Service Centre](https://www.qld.gov.au/housing/public-community-housing/housing-service-centre/) or call 13QGOV (13 74 68) to discuss your specific circumstances as we may be able to help you access other services.

name: Safe Places for Children
description: Safe Places for Children provides transitional and short to medium-term housing for young people in care.

name: Open Doors Youth Service
description: Open Doors Youth Service provides housing help and social support for young people with diverse genders, sexualities and bodies.

name: Rent Assistance
description: Rent Assistance is a regular payment you may be entitled to from Services Australia (Centrelink) to help you pay your rent. This service is provided by the Australian Government. For information on who can get rent assistance, visit [Rent Assistance](https://www.humanservices.gov.au/individuals/services/centrelink/rent-assistance).

name: Residential Tenancies Authority (RTA)
description: The RTA is a Queensland Government statutory authority that helps make renting work for everyone. We work with all groups including tenants, property managers and owners, community and industry stakeholder groups and the Queensland Government.

name: Tenancy disputes
description: The Residential Tenancies Authority (RTA) provides a free, confidential dispute resolution service to help tenants and property managers/owners if they are unable to resolve tenancy disputes themselves.

name: Bond refunds and lodgements
description: A rental bond is a security deposit paid at the start of a tenancy. If a property manager/owner takes a bond, they must give the tenant a receipt and lodge it with the RTA within 10 days. It is an offence not to do so. The bond is paid back to the tenant when the property is vacated, provided no money is owed for rent, damages or other costs.

name: Tenants Queensland
description: Tenants Queensland offers a free statewide advice service for tenants. It seeks to represent the concerns of all Queensland tenants who live in private rental accommodation, including caravan park and boarding house residents.

name: No interest loan scheme
description: No Interest Loans are only available in the Cairns and Gold Coast areas. These loans are for Queenslanders who need extra support to set up or continue a rental tenancy.

name: Help to manage your finances
description: The Queensland Financial Resilience Program is a free service that can help you to better manage your money and be more financially secure.

name: Saving for a home
description: Buying a house is exciting and life-changing. It all starts with saving for the deposit and the more money you put down upfront, the less you'll have to borrow. There are many ways to save for a home. With a good savings plan and some discipline, you'll soon have the deposit for your home.

name: First Home Super Saver Scheme
description: The First Home Super Saver (FHSS) Scheme was introduced to reduce pressure on housing affordability and allows you to save money for a first home inside your superannuation fund. This will help first home buyers save faster with the concessional tax treatment within super.

name: Home Assist Secure
description: Home Assist Secure can provide safety related information, referrals and subsidised assistance if you are unable to undertake or pay for critical maintenance services to your home.

name: Queensland Civil and Administrative Tribunal (QCAT)
description: QCAT is an independent, accessible tribunal that efficiently resolves disputes on a range of matters. The tribunal's purpose is to provide a quick, inexpensive avenue to resolve disputes between parties and make decisions.

name: Pathways Shared Equity Loan
description: The Pathways Shared Equity Loan can help eligible public housing tenants buy a share of the home they are renting.
